parts.title: The Divided Village
parts.text: Long ago, two villages sat on opposite sides of a wide river. Anika lived on the east bank, and Marcus lived on the west. A **dispute** had broken out between the two villages over who owned the bridge. Anika felt a deep **allegiance** to her people and wanted to protect them. Marcus, once her friend, had become her **adversary** in this conflict.

parts.title: The Dangerous Crossing
parts.text: One stormy night, Lin, a young traveler, tried to cross the bridge. The river rose high and placed her in great **peril**. Anika wanted to help, but she was **reluctant** to cross into the other village's territory. She knew she had to **confront** her fear. With great **valor**, Anika stepped onto the shaking bridge and reached for Lin's hand.

parts.title: The Truce
parts.text: Marcus saw what was happening and ran to help. Together, they pulled Lin to safety. The crowd from both villages began to **protest**, shouting that no one should cross the bridge. But Lin's rescue started to **overwhelm** people with a new feeling — kindness. Marcus decided to **surrender** the argument and offered peace. From his **standpoint**, saving a life mattered more than winning. The villagers agreed to **retreat** from their angry positions.

parts.title: A New Beginning
parts.text: The next morning, both villages gathered on the bridge. Anika and Marcus shook hands while Lin smiled between them. The bridge was no longer a line that divided people — it was a place that brought them together. From that day on, the two villages shared the bridge and celebrated their friendship every spring.

definitions.meaning1: To face something or someone in a bold or direct way. Example: She decided to confront the bully instead of hiding from him.
definitions.studentChallenge: It took courage to ___ the problem instead of running away from it.

definitions.meaning1: A disagreement or argument between people. Example: The two friends had a dispute about which movie to watch.
definitions.meaning2: To argue or disagree with something. Example: She wanted to dispute the unfair rule at school.
definitions.studentChallenge: The neighbors had a ___ over where the fence should be built.

definitions.meaning1: Serious and immediate danger. Example: The hikers were in great peril when the storm hit the mountain.
definitions.studentChallenge: The sailors faced great ___ as the waves crashed against their tiny boat.

definitions.meaning1: Not willing or eager to do something. Example: He was reluctant to jump into the cold pool on a chilly day.
definitions.studentChallenge: She was ___ to try the new food because it looked strange.

definitions.meaning1: To give up or stop fighting. Example: The losing team had to surrender the trophy to the winners.
definitions.meaning2: The act of giving up. Example: The soldiers raised a white flag as a sign of surrender.
definitions.studentChallenge: After a long game of tug-of-war, the other team had to ___ and let go of the rope.

definitions.meaning1: Great courage and bravery, especially in battle or danger. Example: The firefighter showed true valor when she rescued the family from the burning building.
definitions.studentChallenge: The knight was honored for his ___ in protecting the kingdom.

definitions.meaning1: An opponent or enemy. Example: In the chess tournament, her toughest adversary was the boy from the other school.
definitions.studentChallenge: The two teams were friendly off the field, but during the game, each was the other's ___.

definitions.meaning1: Loyalty or devotion to a person, group, or cause. Example: The citizens showed their allegiance to their country by flying flags on the holiday.
definitions.studentChallenge: The knight pledged his ___ to the queen and promised to serve her faithfully.

definitions.meaning1: To have a very strong effect on someone's feelings. Example: The surprise party was so nice that joy began to overwhelm her.
definitions.meaning2: To defeat completely. Example: The flood waters threatened to overwhelm the small town.
definitions.studentChallenge: The amount of homework started to ___ the students before the holiday break.

definitions.meaning1: To speak out against something you think is wrong. Example: The students decided to protest the unfair dress code by writing letters to the principal.
definitions.meaning2: A public event where people show they disagree with something. Example: The town held a peaceful protest to save the old library.
definitions.studentChallenge: The workers planned to ___ the new rules they thought were unfair.

definitions.meaning1: To move back or away from something. Example: The campers had to retreat from the river when the water started rising.
definitions.meaning2: A quiet place to rest and relax. Example: The cabin in the woods was a peaceful retreat from the noisy city.
definitions.studentChallenge: When the thunder got louder, the dog tried to ___ under the bed.

definitions.meaning1: A way of thinking about something; a point of view. Example: From her standpoint, the science project was the most important assignment of the year.
definitions.studentChallenge: From the teacher's ___, homework helps students practice what they learned in class.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The two neighbors had a _______ over whose tree was dropping leaves into the other's yard.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The siblings got into a _______ about who got to sit in the front seat of the car.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The firefighter showed great _______ by running into the burning building to save the cat.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The soldier received a medal for her _______ on the battlefield.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She was _______ to ride the roller coaster because the drop looked so steep.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The puppy was _______ to go outside during the thunderstorm.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: When the bees came too close, the picnic group decided to _______ to the car.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The army had to _______ when the river flooded the valley they were crossing.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The students wanted to _______ the decision to cancel recess by writing a letter to the principal.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The crowd gathered to _______ the plan to cut down the oldest tree in the park.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The swimmers were in great _______ when the lifeguard spotted a strong current pulling them out.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The tiny kitten was in _______ as it clung to the top of the tall fence.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She decided to _______ the bully and tell him to stop being mean to her friends.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: It's not easy to _______ a difficult problem, but avoiding it only makes things worse.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: After losing every piece, the chess player had no choice but to _______ the game.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The captain refused to _______ even when the other ship was much bigger.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: In the spelling bee, her toughest _______ was the boy who had won three years in a row.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The superhero's greatest _______ was the villain who lived in the dark castle.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: From the coach's _______, the team needed more practice before the big game.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: From a safety _______, wearing a helmet while biking is always a good idea.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The citizens showed their _______ to the flag by placing their hands over their hearts.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Her _______ to her best friend meant she would always stand by her side.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The surprise birthday party seemed to _______ her with happiness, and she burst into tears of joy.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: So many assignments at once can _______ even the best students in the class.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
In the year 1820, two towns along the Gold River argued over who owned the only bridge. The _(1)_ had lasted for months, and no one could agree. Anika, the youngest town leader, felt a deep _(2)_ to her people. She wanted to protect them, but she was _(3)_ to start a fight.

One cold night, a terrible flood put the bridge in great _(4)_. Marcus, the leader from the other town and Anika's greatest _(5)_, saw the danger too. Anika decided to _(6)_ the problem head-on. She crossed the river with great _(7)_, wading through freezing water to reach the other side.

Marcus wanted to _(8)_ her plan at first, saying it was too risky. But the rising water began to _(9)_ the bridge, cracking its wooden beams. From Marcus's _(10)_, working together was the only choice. He decided to _(11)_ his pride and help Anika. Together, they saved the bridge and called on both towns to _(12)_ from the argument and live in peace.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
